signals.headline: The Dallas Mavericks defeated the Los Angeles Lakers 134-128 on Sunday night (April 5, 2026), with rookie Cooper Flagg s
signals.raw: The Dallas Mavericks defeated the Los Angeles Lakers 134-128 on Sunday night (April 5, 2026), with rookie Cooper Flagg scoring 45 points, 9 assists, and 8 rebounds—his second straight 40+ point game after 51 points two nights prior, a feat matching Allen Iverson as the first rookie since 1996-97.[1][2][3][4]
matched_markets: Will Cooper Flagg win the 2025–26 NBA Rookie of the Year award?

signals.headline: This victory ends Dallas's longest home losing streak in 32 years (14 games, first home win since Jan
signals.raw: This victory ends Dallas's longest home losing streak in 32 years (14 games, first home win since Jan. 22 vs. Warriors); Lakers were "injury-depleted," impacting their performance (29% from three vs. Mavs' 44%).[1][3][4]
